# UC-22.15.7 · OT Security Audit Population Sampling Dashboard

## Description

Delivers repeatable audit samples of OT security-relevant events across zones for internal and external assessments of the security program.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Land firewall, HMI, PAM, IDS into one auditable index with RBAC; (2) maintain CIDR→zone lookup; (3) export monthly CSV to auditors; (4) document sampling method in scope letter. Indexes required: `index=ot_audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), CIM Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_audit` unified; `ot_zones.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Land firewall, HMI, PAM, IDS into one auditable index with RBAC; (2) maintain CIDR→zone lookup; (3) export monthly CSV to auditors; (4) document sampling method in scope letter.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_audit earliest=-90d
| lookup ot_zones.csv cidr OUTPUT zone, sl_t
| stats count as evt, dc(user) as users, dc(dest_ip) as peers by zone, sourcetype
| sort zone, - evt
| head 300
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Security Audit Population Sampling Dashboard (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.4.3)** — Delivers repeatable audit samples of OT security-relevant events across zones for internal and external assessments of the security program.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_audit` unified; `ot_zones.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), CIM Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT firewall and the approved zone-to-zone matrix for that window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (zone × sourcetype), Table, Bar (evt by zone).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_audit earliest=-90d
| lookup ot_zones.csv cidr OUTPUT zone, sl_t
| stats count as evt, dc(user) as users, dc(dest_ip) as peers by zone, sourcetype
| sort zone, - evt
| head 300
```

## Visualization

Heatmap (zone × sourcetype), Table, Bar (evt by zone).
